en.name: Peripheral blood specimen
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Peripheral blood specimen is a sample of blood obtained from a peripheral vessel (typically a peripheral vein), as opposed to a central catheter, an artery, or umbilical / cord blood. It is the most common source of blood samples for routine laboratory testing. In SNOMED CT, this concept is sibling to `Arterial blood specimen` and is positioned to encode the **anatomical site** of the draw, complementary to the specimen-type concepts (whole blood, plasma, serum).

**OMOP CDM usage.** This concept set targets the `SPECIMEN.specimen_concept_id` field. See the [Mapping Recommendations](#/mapping-recommendations) — *Lab Mapping → Specimen* section for how MEASUREMENT and SPECIMEN are linked in CDM v5.4.

## Included Concepts

The set is anchored on the SNOMED Specimen concept `Peripheral blood specimen`, expanded to include all its descendants. The set resolves to one specimen:

- *4047495 / 122551003 — Peripheral blood specimen (SNOMED)*

## Excluded Concepts

No concepts are explicitly excluded by name. The set deliberately omits:

- **Cord, umbilical, fetal blood specimens**: physiologically distinct from post-natal peripheral blood.
- **Central venous, arterial samples**: covered by the separate "Venous blood specimen" or "Arterial blood specimen" concept sets.

## Mapping Notes

- **Default mapping** when the source data records a peripheral venipuncture (e.g. routine biology draw, antecubital vein): `4047495 / 122551003 — Peripheral blood specimen`.
- **Source records a sample drawn from an artery, central line, umbilicus or cord**: do **not** map. Use the appropriate dedicated concept set.
- **Do not confuse with**: `Whole blood specimen` (specimen type, anticoagulant-related), which describes the **physical state** of the sample, not the **anatomical site**.
